channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Netflix misspelled a lead Devil May Cry character's name and had to recall official merchandise
item.description: The t-shirt in question refers to Dante and "Virgil."

item.title: Netflix’s ad ambitions just keep growing
item.description: For the second year in a row, Netflix's ad-supported tier more than doubled its monthly viewerbase. During its 2026 upfront presentation, Netflix revealed that its $8.99-per-month plan reaches more than 250 million people around the globe, a significant leap from 94 million users reported last year. The streaming service reported earning $1.5 billion from ads […]...

item.title: Threads is adding a Grok-like AI search feature
item.description: Threads is adding a new Meta Spark feature that's similar to X's Grok AI

item.title: Everything at The Criterion Collection is 30 percent off right now
item.description: If you’ve been meaning to grow your physical movie collection, now’s a good time to do it. Through May 25th, The Criterion Collection is taking 30 percent off every disc on its site, including standard Blu-rays, 4K releases, box sets, and even films that are currently only available to preorder. The sale spans every category […]

item.title: Xbox's new filters aim to make sense of disorganized game libraries
item.description: And, brace yourself, Microsoft is also testing a new startup experience.

item.title: OpenAI endorses the Kids Online Safety Act
item.description: The bill appears to be gaining new momentum.

item.title: This is what some the world’s largest banks of malware look like stacked as hard drives
item.description: What would some of the world's largest repositories of malware look like if they were stacked as hard drives, one on top of the other?

item.title: Overworked AI Agents Turn Marxist, Researchers Find
item.description: In a recent experiment, mistreated AI agents started grumbling about inequality and calling for collective bargaining rights.

item.title: LinkedIn is reportedly laying off five percent of its workforce
item.description: Marketing, engineering and product teams are among those affected, according to a leaked memo.

item.title: Meta workers revolt against mouse tracking technology — flyers ask if they want to work at 'the Employee Data ​Extraction Factory'
item.description: Privacy concerns aside, Meta workers are worried that AI-training mouse tracking software will ultimately lead to further job cuts.

item.title: iOS 27 will bring updates to key apps on your iPhone, report claims, but don’t expect big Liquid Glass changes
item.description: A new report claims iOS 27 will see big changes to Apple’s iPhone apps, including Liquid Glass adjustments.

item.title: Microsoft reveals another way it's making Windows 11 faster, with more performance boosts promised for the likes of File Explorer
item.description: 'It's a long-term commitment for fundamentals and quality': Microsoft is working to make Windows 11 faster in multiple ways, including in-depth honing of the interface.

item.title: One major theater chain just quietly launched $1.75 movie tickets for summer break
item.description: The nationwide promotion also includes discounts on popcorn, drinks, and snack packs. Cinemark is giving customers a break at the box office this summer. The movie chain that operates over 300 theaters in the U.S. just announced it’s offering a major deal on tickets as part of its Summer Movie Clubhouse program.

item.title: Instagram hits the copy button again with new disappearing Instants photos
item.description: Instagram is once again cribbing from competitors like Snapchat and BeReal with a new photo-sharing format it calls "Instants," which are ephemeral photos that you can't edit and that you can only share with your close friends or followers that follow you back. Instants are available globally beginning on Wednesday as a feature in the […]

item.title: Google could work with SpaceX to launch its orbital data centers
item.description: Google and SpaceX are reportedly in talks for a deal to launch AI data centers into space.

item.title: A ‘cosmic triangle’ will appear in the sky tonight: When and where to see Saturn, Mars, and the moon align in May 2026
item.description: Skywatchers are in for a treat this week, as the planets will put on a rare show in the early morning hours. You won’t want to miss a chance to look up tonight into the sky, in the early morning hours on Thursday, May 14. Before dawn, skywatchers are in for a treat with a rare sighting of the moon, Saturn, and Mars as they a form a gorgeous, cosmic triangle in May’s dark sky....

item.title: Kindle Colorsoft, Samsung Galaxy Z Flip FE et TV Philips Ambilight : les baisses de prix les plus irrésistibles du jour — meilleurs bons plans
item.description: Ce mercredi 13 mai 2026, la tech est à la fête avec trois offres majeures. Entre la Kindle Colorsoft à prix bas sur Amazon, un Galaxy Z Flip FE enfin abordable et un TV Philips QLED 144 Hz bradé, c'est le moment idéal pour s'équiper.

item.title: Geothermal startup Fervo Energy pops 33% in IPO debut fueled by AI data center demand
item.description: Fervo Energy's IPO was upsized several times after potential investors asked why the enhanced geothermal startup wasn't raising more money.

item.title: Meta employees are protesting the company's mouse tracking program
item.description: Last month, it said it would use employees' mouse movements and keystrokes to train AI models.

item.title: Why change doesn’t really come from the top
item.description: What truly matters? Whether stakeholders are on board with the transformation. In early 2000, with their company on the brink of failure, Netflix founders Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph flew to Dallas to meet with Blockbuster executives. As the story is told, they offered to sell their company for $50 million and got laughed out of the room. Humiliated, but determined, they built a business that toppled the industry giant. ...

item.title: Slice over $300 off the Mammotion Luba mini 2 robot lawn mower and take back your time this season
item.description: The Mammotion Luba Mini 2 robot lawn mower is on sale at Amazon for $1,899 instead of $2,208 and it comes with a mini garage.

item.title: Instagram debuts a new disappearing-photo app called Instants
item.description: It's all about sharing candid snaps in the moment.

item.title: How to watch Eurovision 2026 Semi-Final 2 online for FREE
item.description: 15 more countries go head-to-head in the second semi-final of Eurovision Song Contest 2026 in Vienna. Here's how to watch Eurovision 2026 Semi-Final 2 online and for free from anywhere.

item.title: X launches a History tab for bookmarks, likes, videos, and articles
item.description: X’s new History tab combines bookmarks, likes, watched videos, and read articles into a single place, expanding the app’s role as a save-it-for-later tool.

item.title: Messaging app Tokee may have leaked 1.2 million user profiles — experts say exposed personal data 'presents significant privacy, security, and regulatory risks'
item.description: Most of the app's userbase seems to have been exposed, so users should be on their guard.

item.title: OpenAI Brings Its Ass to Court
item.description: In Musk v. Altman, the company sought to show the jury a remarkable trophy as physical proof of Elon Musk’s concerning behavior.

item.title: Nvidia franchit les 5 500 milliards en Bourse, du jamais-vu dans l’histoire
item.description: Nvidia a franchi les 5 500 milliards de dollars de capitalisation boursière ce mercredi 13 mai 2026, un sommet que personne n'avait jamais atteint. Le fabricant californien de puces pour l'IA (et accessoirement des cartes graphiques pour les gamers) vaut désormais plus d'une fois et demie le PIB annuel de la France.

item.title: I delved into the wild world of pet tech to find the 9 best cat-care gadgets — including a feeder with facial recognition, and a self-cleaning litter box with a sealed 'poop dungeon'
item.description: Smart gadgets to take the burden off kitty care while you're out of the house.

item.title: Vapes and e-bikes mean UK firefighters now deal with a lithium-ion battery fire every five hours — follow these tips to stay safe
item.description: A new report highlights a growing problem with lithium-ion fires, and modified e-bikes in particular.
